vBRIEFInfo.author: planning-agent:claude-opus-4-5
vBRIEFInfo.description: Plan for PAN-1044: Command Deck — Project Overview Panel with pipeline swimlanes. Empirically scoped 2026-05-08 — see docs/prds/planned/PAN-1044-project-overview-panel.md.

plan.title: Command Deck: Project Overview Panel with pipeline swimlanes
plan.author: planning-agent:claude-opus-4-5

narratives.summary: Add a Project Overview panel to the Command Deck right content area. Clicking a project name (not the chevron) selects the project and shows a hero billboard, stuck/blocked callout, and pipeline swimlane board. All data is available client-side — no new API endpoints.
narratives.decisions: D1: SessionNode.type (not role) classifies session types — PRD erroneously used s.role === 'work'. D2: issueCosts is Record<string,number> not Map. D3: feature.issueId is uppercase, reviewStatusByIssueId keys are uppercase — direct lookup is safe. D4: Use useDashboardStore(s => s.reviewStatusByIssueId) once at component top, not in a loop. D5: Export bucketFeature for testability, tests co-located in B2.
narratives.hazards: H1: ProjectNode is currently one <button> — split with chevron <span onClick e.stopPropagation> inside the button. H2: issueCosts must be passed to ProjectOverview explicitly in routing block. H3: projectsWithSessions is ProjectData[] — use .find(p => p.name === selectedProject)?.features ?? [].

narrative.Action: File docs/prds/planned/PAN-1044-project-overview-panel.md already created by planning agent. Mark bead workspace-ma73 complete.

subItems.title: File exists at docs/prds/planned/PAN-1044-project-overview-panel.md
Codebase corrections documented (SessionNode.type, Record types, uppercase issueId)

narrative.Action: Create ProjectOverview.tsx with: (1) exported bucketFeature(feature, reviewStatus) and PipelineStage type — see PRD for 9-stage bucketing logic with s.type === 'work' correction. (2) Hero billboard: background gradient, project name, issue count, total cost via LiveCounter, active agent count. (3) useDashboardStore(s => s.reviewStatusByIssueId) at top of component. (4) ProjectOverviewProps: { projectName, features: ProjectFeature[], issueCosts: Record<string, number>, onSelectFeature }. Create ProjectOverview.test.tsx covering all 9 bucketFeature stage branches (stuck via stuck flag, stuck via failed status, stuck via blockerReasons, merging, awaitingMerge, tests, review, buildGate, working, planning, idle).

subItems.title: ProjectOverview.tsx renders hero billboard with project name, issue count, total cost (LiveCounter), active agent count
bucketFeature exported, returns correct PipelineStage for all 9 inputs
ProjectOverview.test.tsx covers all 9 stage branches of bucketFeature
npm run typecheck and npm test pass

narrative.Action: Complete ProjectOverview.tsx: (1) Add stuck/blocked callout section at top with AlertTriangle icon, var(--destructive) accent, only shown when stuck issues exist. Each stuck card shows: Issue ID (clickable onSelectFeature), title truncated 1 line, stuck reason (stuckReason or blockerReasons[0] or failed status name). (2) Add pipeline swimlane sections — one Section per non-empty stage. Header: stage name + count badge. Body: horizontal flex-wrap of issue cards. Cards: Issue ID (clickable), title truncated (12px), cost badge if cost exists, sub-status pill (review: reviewSubStatuses roles, merge: mergeStep, buildGate: verificationCycleCount if > 1). Empty stages hidden. All colors via CSS variables only.

subItems.title: Stuck/blocked callout appears only when stuck issues exist, with AlertTriangle icon
Pipeline swimlane sections rendered, one per non-empty stage
Empty pipeline stages are hidden
Clicking issue card in any swimlane calls onSelectFeature with correct feature
All colors use CSS variables only (no hardcoded values)
npm run typecheck and npm test pass

items.title: ProjectNode.tsx — split click behavior and selection visual

narrative.Action: Modify ProjectTree/ProjectNode.tsx: (1) Add props: onSelectProject?: (projectName: string) => void and selectedProject?: string | null. (2) Split click targets: wrap ChevronRight in <span onClick={(e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setExpanded(!expanded); }} style={{ cursor: 'pointer', display: 'inline-flex', alignItems: 'center' }}>. Outer button onClick: calls onSelectProject?.(name) AND if (!expanded) setExpanded(true). (3) Apply style={{ background: selectedProject === name ? 'var(--accent)' : undefined }} to the projectHeader button. MessageSquarePlus e.stopPropagation() must be preserved.

subItems.title: Clicking chevron only toggles expand/collapse, does NOT select the project
Clicking project name row calls onSelectProject and expands if collapsed
Selected project row shows var(--accent) background
MessageSquarePlus button still works with e.stopPropagation()
npm run typecheck passes

items.title: CommandDeck/index.tsx — selectedProject state and four-way routing

narrative.Action: Modify CommandDeck/index.tsx: (1) Add state: const [selectedProject, setSelectedProject] = useState<string | null>(null). (2) Add handler: const handleSelectProject = useCallback((projectName: string) => { setSelectedProject(projectName); setSelectedFeature(null); setSelectedConversation(null); }, []). (3) Update handleSelectFeature to add setSelectedProject(null). (4) Update handleSelectConversation to add setSelectedProject(null). (5) Pass onSelectProject={handleSelectProject} and selectedProject={selectedProject} to each ProjectNode. (6) In content area routing, add four-way: conversation -> ConversationView, feature -> IssueWorkbench, project -> ProjectOverview (with projectName={selectedProject}, features={projectsWithSessions.find(p => p.name === selectedProject)?.features ?? []}, issueCosts={issueCosts}, onSelectFeature={feature => handleSelectFeature(feature.issueId)}), empty -> EmptyState. Import ProjectOverview from './ProjectOverview'.

subItems.title: Selecting a project shows ProjectOverview in the content area
Selecting a feature clears selectedProject
Selecting a conversation clears selectedProject
Four-way routing priority: conversation > feature > project > empty
Clicking issue card in ProjectOverview navigates to that issue's IssueWorkbench
npm run typecheck, npm run lint, and npm test all pass
